summary: Full e2e testing of JARVIS personal assistant (backend + frontend). All 7 backend pytest cases passed (identity, empty validation, send_sms, set_alarm, web_search, identity Q, persistence/memory/history clear). Frontend loads correctly, message send + action card render + msg-count increment + clear-history all working.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 173-178: prior history is fetched AFTER inserting the new user message and then filtered out by id; works correctly but inserting before LLM call risks orphan user-only rows if LLM call fails (the 502 leaves the user msg persisted without a paired jarvis reply). Consider inserting user msg only after a successful LLM round-trip, or include a status field.

server.py: LlmChat is instantiated fresh each request without replaying prior turns through the library's message API. Memory is recreated by prepending a compact transcript into the user message — works but couples context length to message size. Acceptable for MVP.
ChatResponse 'action' is free-form Dict (per agent note) — model returns flat keys (to/message) rather than nested params. Tests tolerated both. Consider documenting the schema.
No rate limiting or auth on /api/jarvis/* endpoints — anyone can hit Karim's session. Out of scope for MVP but worth flagging.
seed_data_creation: none; tests create and clean their own per-session data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 via emergentintegrations LlmChat. Action JSON returns flat shape (e.g. {type:'send_sms', to:'Rakib', message:'...'}) NOT wrapped in params{} — tests check both. Frontend session_id is 'karim-default'; tests use isolated test-<uuid> session ids and clean up. Voice input untested (headless browsers lack SpeechRecognition — by design).
